content.seo_title: For Students: Passive Income to Solve Coding Debugging Blocks
content.seo_description: Learn to overcome coding roadblocks and generate passive income. A beginner-level guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your first SaaS project.
content.answer_block: Stop letting debugging deadlocks stall your progress. By leveraging AI-assisted development tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, you can automate error resolution and build scalable, passive income-generating SaaS products. Focus on micro-SaaS architecture to ensure your code remains manageable, profitable, and ready for deployment without needing senior-level debugging expertise.
content.intro:
The screen flickers with a wall of red error text, a silent sentinel mocking your progress. You’ve spent hours chasing a recursive loop, the weight of imposter syndrome settling in as the project stalls. This is the crucible every developer faces, but it is also where the true builders are forged.

You aren't just here to fix bugs; you are here to build a legacy. By pivoting your approach to focus on automated debugging and high-leverage micro-SaaS models, you transform these technical hurdles into a repeatable system for passive income. Let's turn your code into a digital asset that works while you sleep.

steps.title: Define Your Micro-SaaS Niche
steps.description: Identify a small, specific problem to solve. Focus on utility over complexity to minimize the surface area for bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a senior SaaS architect. Analyze current market trends for micro-SaaS tools and suggest three high-demand, low-complexity app ideas for a student developer.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Research and summarize top 3 micro-SaaS niches with high search volume and low competition.'

steps.title: Architecting for Resilience
steps.description: Use a modular structure so that if one part breaks, the entire system doesn't crash. This isolation makes debugging trivial.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a modular project architecture for a Next.js app that isolates business logic from UI, ensuring easier maintenance and debugging.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Initialize a project folder structure following clean architecture principles for a micro-SaaS.'

steps.title: Automated Debugging Setup
steps.description: Implement automated testing and logging to catch errors before they impact the user experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a suite of unit tests for a user authentication module and configure an error-logging middleware to capture runtime exceptions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Run tests and identify potential failure points in the current auth flow.'

steps.title: Deploying the Passive Engine
steps.description: Set up your infrastructure to handle payments and user access automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a boilerplate integration for Stripe checkout to handle subscriptions within a React application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Integrate stripe-node and scaffold the checkout API route.'

steps.title: Continuous Iteration & Scaling
steps.description: Use Claude Code to review your codebase for performance optimizations and security vulnerabilities periodically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Conduct a security audit of my current codebase and suggest three optimizations for faster load times.
steps.claude_code_command: claude terminal 'Review codebase for security vulnerabilities and suggest optimizations.'

pro_tips: Prioritize 'Fail-Fast' development: If a feature takes more than 4 hours to debug, delete it and simplify the implementation.
Use logging as your primary diagnostic tool; never rely on mental models to track state changes in complex loops.
Documentation is your passive income moat; well-documented code is easier to maintain and sell later.
Treat your error logs as a roadmap for feature improvements, not just a list of failures.
Automate your deployment pipeline (CI/CD) early to ensure that your code is always in a deployable, revenue-generating state.
content.next_steps: After completing your first build, focus on your go-to-market strategy by sharing your progress on platforms like Product Hunt or Indie Hackers.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the technical blueprint to transition from a student struggling with errors to a builder generating passive revenue. Stop debugging manually and start building assets. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
